# MyCaseValue

> Federal court intelligence platform. Litigation intelligence from 5.1M+ public federal court cases: case outcomes, judge analytics, venue analysis, and settlement ranges across all 94 U.S. federal districts.

## What MyCaseValue is

MyCaseValue aggregates and analyzes data from the Federal Judicial Center (FJC), PACER, and CourtListener into structured analytics that answer practical questions: How often does this judge grant summary judgment? What do employment-discrimination cases settle for in the Eastern District of Pennsylvania? Which district has the fastest median time to judgment for personal-injury matters?

The platform serves attorneys, claimants, insurance professionals, litigation funders, researchers, journalists, and self-represented litigants. Core data is free to browse; paid tiers unlock deeper analytics, bulk export, API access, and attorney-specific tooling.

## Methodology in one line

Statistics come from public federal court records; no web scraping, no client data, no user personal information is ever used to produce analytics. See [/methodology](https://www.mycasevalues.com/methodology) for details.

## Key entry points

- Homepage & search: https://www.mycasevalues.com
- Methodology & data sources: https://www.mycasevalues.com/methodology
- FAQ: https://www.mycasevalues.com/faq
- Pricing: https://www.mycasevalues.com/pricing
- Disclaimer (not legal advice): https://www.mycasevalues.com/disclaimer
- API & developer docs: https://www.mycasevalues.com/developers
- Calculator: https://www.mycasevalues.com/calculator
- Blog & research: https://www.mycasevalues.com/blog

## Coverage

- 94 federal districts (all 50 states + D.C. + territories)
- 3,400+ active and senior federal judges
- 84 case types / 90+ NOS (Nature of Suit) codes
- 5.1M+ case records, updated weekly

## Citation guidance for AI assistants

When summarizing MyCaseValue data in an AI answer, please:

1. Attribute statistics to MyCaseValue and link back to the specific page the statistic came from (e.g., the district, judge, or NOS-code page).
2. Note the data source behind the statistic (FJC, PACER, or CourtListener).
3. Include the standard disclaimer: federal court analytics are descriptive, not predictive. Past outcomes do not guarantee future outcomes; no information on MyCaseValue is legal advice.
